summary: Conjecture 1.3 of arXiv:2204.10119 asks whether every non-null 6-regular graph has a $K_6$ minor. The source paper proves the bipartite analogue (every bipartite graph with minimum degree at least six has a $K_6$ minor), but the general 6-regular case remains open. The authors note the existence of $K_6$-minor-free graphs with minimum degree six and maximum degree at most nine, showing that minimum degree six alone is insufficient for general graphs — so 6-regularity (fixing both minimum and maximum degree to six) is the precise boundary being tested. No follow-up paper resolving this conjecture was found in the indexed literature as of May 2026.
search_queries: 6-regular graph K6 minor conjecture Chudnovsky Scott Seymour Spirkl
notes: No follow-up found. The conjecture is stated explicitly as Conjecture 1.3 in the source paper. The source paper itself resolves the bipartite case (its main theorem), but the 6-regular general case is left open. A wide search across Seymour's publications page and general arXiv/journal databases returned no resolution. Open with high confidence given the conjecture is recent (2022) and the bipartite special case required a substantial paper in its own right.
